summary.prompt: Build me a kanban board with multiple lists, drag-and-drop, and local saving.
summary.raw_verdict: delivered. All 6 required artifacts present. CV pass, Critic clean (one low-severity manifest-schema flag, non-blocking). 16/16 edge-case assertions + 8/8 CV user-flow scenarios pass.

ledger.restatement: A single-user, client-side kanban board web application with multiple named lists (columns), cards that can be created and moved between lists via drag-and-drop, and persistent state saved locally in the browser so the board survives page reloads. No backend, no authentication, no collaboration — just one person managing tasks across columns on their own machine.

sections.charter_excerpt: Define the pure-JavaScript data model for the kanban board (board, lists, cards) and provide reducer-style mutation functions that return new state without touching the DOM. Includes id generation, validation, and a subscribe/notify mechan…
sections.acceptance_excerpt: Exposes createList, renameList, deleteList, createCard, editCard, deleteCard, moveCard, reorderCard mutations; getState returns current board; subscribe registers change listeners; mutations validate inputs and produce predictable state tr…

sections.charter_excerpt: Implement a localStorage adapter that loads board state on startup and saves it after every mutation. Exposes load(), save(state), and clear() functions plus a key constant. Handles JSON parse errors and missing data gracefully by returnin…
sections.acceptance_excerpt: load() returns parsed board state or null if absent/corrupt; save(state) serializes state to localStorage under a stable key; clear() removes the key; corrupt JSON does not throw uncaught; survives page reload.

sections.charter_excerpt: Render the current board state into the DOM: lists as columns containing card elements, plus a 'add list' affordance and an 'add card' affordance per list. Subscribes to the state-model and re-renders on change. Card elements carry data-ca…
sections.acceptance_excerpt: render(rootEl, state) produces one column per list, one card element per card, in the correct order; cards have draggable=true and data attributes for ids; subscription causes re-render on state change; rendering an empty board still shows…

sections.charter_excerpt: Wire HTML5 drag-and-drop onto the rendered DOM so the user can drag cards between lists and reorder cards within a list. On drop, compute the source list, target list, and target index, then invoke moveCard or reorderCard on the state-mode…
sections.acceptance_excerpt: Dragging a card from list A and dropping it on list B invokes moveCard with the correct ids and a target index; dragging within the same list invokes reorderCard with the correct target index; dropping outside any drop zone is a no-op; dra…

sections.charter_excerpt: Wire non-DnD UI events to state-model mutations: clicking 'add list' and submitting a name calls createList; clicking a list title to rename calls renameList; clicking list delete calls deleteList; clicking 'add card' and submitting a titl…
sections.acceptance_excerpt: Each of the six interactions (add list, rename list, delete list, add card, edit card, delete card) successfully invokes the corresponding state-model mutation; empty/whitespace-only titles are rejected client-side before mutation; deletes…

sections.charter_excerpt: Provide index.html and the entry-point app.js that boots the application: load persisted state via persistence; if absent, seed To Do / Doing / Done lists per A11; instantiate the state-model with that initial state; mount ui-render onto a…
sections.acceptance_excerpt: Opening index.html in a modern desktop browser shows a working board; first-load shows To Do / Doing / Done with no cards; subsequent loads restore previous state; every mutation is autosaved without an explicit save button.

sections.charter_excerpt: Cover every acceptance assertion with `verifier: edge_case_testing` from the section list. Every section's acceptance_assertions[] is in scope. Produce executable tests (or test-equivalent harness scripts) that exercise the listed scenario…
sections.acceptance_excerpt: All acceptance_assertions from sections 1-6 with verifier=edge_case_testing have a corresponding executable test that passes; tests fail fast and cleanly on regression; coverage report enumerates each assertion id.

inflection_points.topic: Card data model: title-only vs. title + description (and possibly more fields)
Whether lists themselves are draggable for reordering

what_worked: Inline dispatch was the right call for 7 sections. — The architecture's heuristic (≤8 → inline) held up. A single Coordinator agent decomposing → building → integrating → testing was efficient (~12 minutes wall-clock for the build phase) a…

TD's quick-reasoning resolved both inflection points without research. — Both IPs (title-only cards, fixed list order) cleanly satisfied the four-condition rubric: canonical answers, similar branch complexity, no new dependencies, easily r…

Machine-checkable assertions caught zero drift this run, but they cost almost nothing to write and gave Critic something concrete to grep for. — All 6 IP assertions (3 per IP) verified against the integrated artifact directly via grep + st…

Acceptance assertions with `verifier: cv_artifact_exercise` translated cleanly into a Node+jsdom runner. — CV's user_flow scenarios (first-load seed, reload-survives-add, DnD between lists, delete card, rename list, etc.) ran against the *…

Coordinator self-corrected one test bug during the build. — The original S4.A4 assertion checked `drag-over` removal on the same DOM element after `drop`, but `drop` triggers a re-render that replaces the list element. Coordinator updated …
what_broke: Manifest schema drift was caught only at Critic final-sweep, severity low. — `output/integration/manifest.json` used `created_at` instead of `completed_at` and omitted `section_outputs_used`. Cosmetic, doesn't block delivery, but it's a sm…

No Researcher was dispatched the entire run. — TD resolved both IPs via quick reasoning; Critic flagged nothing escalation-worthy; CV passed. So Arbiter, Researcher (planning + escalation), and Discovery (amendment mode) charters did not e…

DnD reorder-within-list cannot be fully simulated in jsdom. — jsdom doesn't perform CSS layout, so `getBoundingClientRect()` returns zeros and the drop-index calculation degrades. CV's user_flow scenario (d) covered the contract layer (`re…

events.verbatim_excerpt: Sections file has machine_checkable_assertions[] on both IP1 and IP2 (verified earlier in conversation analysis: 6 IP assertions across 2 IPs).
Sections file has 39 acceptance_assertions[] across 7 sections per the run-report. Each prose acceptance phrase has structured assertions.
output/verification/cv-runner.js + cv-runner-report.json exist. 8/8 user_flow scenarios pass under jsdom-based artifact-exercise.

cv-runner-report shows DOM-loaded exercise of integrated artifact with state-model + persistence + ui-render + dnd + interactions. Kanban has zero CDN deps and uses only browser-native localStorage; jsdom natively provides localStorage, so 'no substitution' trivially holds (nothing to substitute). However, the v1.5 amendment was not in force when this run was built, and no explicit production-fidelity declaration appears in the cv-runner-report. The exercise was probably production-fidelity by …

No PNV in either verification report. The prompt's named verb 'organize tasks' (or whatever the literal verb is — 'use a kanban board'?) is not asserted end-to-end. The 8 user_flow scenarios cover individual interactions but no single scenario is the canonical user-success path.
TD's sections file does not include prompt_verb_analysis. The verb-derivation rationale is undocumented.
output/final/ contents look to match output/integration/ (no recovery patches were needed). Divergence record not required.
history/log.jsonl exists; build-summary.md and decision-index.json do not.
Both IPs were Discovery-sourced. No TD-introduced IPs to schema-check.

Run-report mentions Coordinator self-corrected the S4.A4 test specification mid-build. v1.4 requires this to be logged in state/inline-deviations/. Audit needs to verify whether the directory exists and contains an entry for that correction.

This is closer to v1.5 production fidelity than any other prior run. The pass-with-concerns is a documentation gap, not a substantive verification weakness.
